name: Cascada del río Barbantino – circular from O Viñao
name_original: Cascada del río Barbantino – circular desde O Viñao
region_intro: Jogging routes around Punxín, located in the province of Ourense, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, natural monuments, and thermal springs. The region features the Barbantiño River with its notable waterfall, and paths along the Miño River. These environments offer varied terrain for runners, from riverside trails to routes exploring natural pools. Best jogging routes around Punxín The most popular jogging route is Arenteiro Trail - O Carballiño Loop, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Arenteiro River, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada del río Barbantino – circular from O Viñao, a moderate 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This route leads to the Barbantiño Waterfall, providing a natural highlight. Local runners also love the Roman Bridge Ourense loop from Ourense-Empalme, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through riverside areas, often completed in about 46 minutes. Jogging around Punxín is defined by river paths along the Barbantiño and Miño rivers, natural pools, and diverse landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate routes. The routes in Punxín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Punxín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Punxín?
A: Punxín offers a selection of 9 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails explore the region's natural beauty, including river valleys and thermal areas.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Punxín?
A: Yes, Punxín has routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40301077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arenteiro loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4 km (2.7 miles) path, perfect for a gentle jog through pleasant scenery.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to natural attractions like waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its natural features. You can explore routes that lead to the impressive Barbantiño Waterfall. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991641078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del río Barbantino – circular from O Viñao&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you directly to this natural highlight.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails around Punxín?
A: Jogging routes in Punxín offer diverse and picturesque scenery. You'll find yourself running along riverbanks, through lush green landscapes, and past natural pools. The area is characterized by the Barbantiño and Miño rivers, providing a tranquil and scenic backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Punxín area?
A: Yes, many of the running paths in Punxín are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991279665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenteiro Trail - O Carballiño Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route that follows the Arenteiro River, offering beautiful views throughout.

Q: Are the running routes in Punxín suitable for families?
A: Many routes in Punxín are suitable for families, especially those looking for an enjoyable outdoor activity. The varied terrain includes easier sections along river paths, making it possible to find a comfortable pace for all family members. Consider routes with less elevation gain for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Punxín?
A: While specific regulations for each trail vary, generally, many natural paths and riverside routes in Punxín are dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there any thermal springs or natural pools near the running paths?
A: Yes, Punxín is famous for its thermal waters. You can find natural pools and thermal springs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt;, located along or near some running routes. These can be a refreshing stop or a relaxing destination after your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Punxín?
A: The running routes in Punxín are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the beautiful natural landscapes, the tranquility of the river paths, and the variety of terrain that makes for an engaging jogging experience.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks I can see while running?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Punxín also offers glimpses into its rich heritage. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6474629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Rock Tombs of Castro de Santa Mariña&lt;/a&gt;, which provide scenic views and a touch of history to your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31890174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Ourense loop from Ourense-Empalme&lt;/a&gt; also passes by notable landmarks.

Q: Where can I find parking for the jogging routes in Punxín?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every route, many trailheads, especially in villages like O Carballiño or O Viñao, offer local parking options. It's advisable to check local maps or signs upon arrival for designated parking spots near your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on Punxín's running routes?
A: The terrain on Punxín's running routes is quite varied. You'll find a mix of riverside paths, some of which are relatively flat, alongside trails that may include gentle ascents and descents, particularly when exploring areas around waterfalls or natural monuments. The surfaces can range from compacted earth to gravel paths.

Q: Are there any routes along the Miño River?
A: Yes, the Miño River is a significant feature of the region, and you can find excellent running opportunities along its banks. Paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Walk Along the Miño River — Ourense&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic and often flat sections, perfect for a refreshing run with beautiful water views.
tips:
In the Carballiño region, between the municipalities of Amoeiro and Maside, is the Barbantino River waterfall, also known as Fervenza do Cachón.
In times of heavy rains, this waterfall, 15 meters high, is a highly recommended visit to enjoy this natural spectacle.
